jobs.title: Ticket 01: Flutter Project Setup + Dependencies
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 1 — Foundation
**Blocked by:** None
**Blocks:** #02, #03, #04

---

## Summary

Initialize the Flutter project, install all dependencies, and create the directory structure.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Create Flutter project

```bash
flutter create --org com.bauhauspomodoro --project-name bauhaus_pomodoro --platforms ios,android .
```

### 2. Add runtime dependencies

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `flutter_riverpod` | State management |
| `riverpod_annotation` | Riverpod annotations |
| `drift` | Local SQLite database (type-safe) |
| `sqlite3_flutter_libs` | SQLite native bindings |
| `path_provider` | App documents directory for DB file |
| `go_router` | Declarative routing |
| `freezed_annotation` | Immutable model annotations |
| `json_annotation` | JSON serialization annotations |
| `google_fonts` | Space Grotesk + Inter typefaces |
| `fl_chart` | Bar charts for efficiency report |
| `uuid` | Generate unique IDs |
| `flutter_local_notifications` | Timer completion alerts |
| `audioplayers` | Session-complete sound effects |
| `shared_preferences` | Simple settings persistence |

### 3. Add dev dependencies

| Package | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `build_runner` | Code generation runner |
| `freezed` | Generates freezed model code |
| `json_serializable` | Generates JSON serialization code |
| `drift_dev` | Generates Drift database code |

> **Note:** `riverpod_generator` conflicts with `drift_dev` on the `analyzer` package. Skip it — use Riverpod providers manually instead.

### 4. Create directory structure

```
lib/
├── main.dart
├── app.dart
├── core/
│   ├── theme/
│   │   ├── bauhaus_theme.dart
│   │   ├── colors.dart
│   │   └── typography.dart
│   ├── router.dart
│   └── widgets/
│       ├── ghost_block.dart
│       ├── bauhaus_app_bar.dart
│       ├── bauhaus_nav_bar.dart
│       ├── bauhaus_button.dart
│       ├── bauhaus_toggle.dart
│       └── heavy_divider.dart
├── data/
│   ├── database/
│   │   └── app_database.dart
│   └── repositories/
│       ├── task_repository.dart
│       ├── session_repository.dart
│       └── settings_repository.dart
├── domain/
│   ├── models/
│   │   ├── pomodoro_task.dart
│   │   ├── pomodoro_session.dart
│   │   ├── timer_state.dart
│   │   └── app_settings.dart
│   └── services/
│       ├── timer_service.dart
│       └── statistics_service.dart
└── features/
    ├── timer/
    │   ├── timer_screen.dart
    │   └── widgets/
    ├── tasks/
    │   ├── tasks_screen.dart
    │   └── widgets/
    ├── stats/
    │   ├── efficiency_report_screen.dart
    │   └── widgets/
    └── settings/
        ├── settings_screen.dart
        └── widgets/
```

### 5. Verify

- `flutter pub get` succeeds with no conflicts
- `flutter analyze` passes
- App builds and runs showing default counter app

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Flutter project created with correct org and platforms
- [ ] All dependencies resolve without conflicts
- [ ] Directory structure created with placeholder files
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes
- [ ] App builds on Android emulator

jobs.title: Ticket 02: Bauhaus Design System + Theme
Ticket 03: Data Models + Drift Database Schema
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 1 — Foundation
**Blocked by:** #01
**Blocks:** #05, #06, #08, #09, #12, #13

---

## Summary

Define all domain models using Freezed and the local SQLite database schema using Drift. This establishes the data layer that the timer, tasks, stats, and settings all build on.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Freezed models

#### `lib/domain/models/pomodoro_task.dart`

```dart
enum TaskPriority { high, secondary, maintenance }
enum TaskStatus { pending, active, completed }

@freezed
class PomodoroTask {
  id: String (UUID)
  title: String                    // e.g. "Refactor Typography Engine"
  description: String              // Task details
  priority: TaskPriority           // high (red), secondary (blue), maintenance (yellow)
  status: TaskStatus
  estimatedPomodoros: int          // How many pomodoros estimated
  completedPomodoros: int          // How many completed so far
  projectName: String?             // Optional project label
  createdAt: DateTime
  completedAt: DateTime?
}
```

#### `lib/domain/models/pomodoro_session.dart`

```dart
enum SessionType { focus, shortBreak, longBreak }

@freezed
class PomodoroSession {
  id: String (UUID)
  taskId: String?                  // FK to task (null for unlinked sessions)
  type: SessionType
  durationSeconds: int             // Configured duration
  startedAt: DateTime
  completedAt: DateTime?           // null if abandoned
  wasCompleted: bool               // Did they finish the full duration?
}
```

#### `lib/domain/models/timer_state.dart`

```dart
enum TimerPhase { idle, focus, shortBreak, longBreak }

@freezed
class TimerState {
  phase: TimerPhase
  remainingSeconds: int
  totalSeconds: int
  currentSession: int              // Which session in the cycle (1-based)
  totalSessions: int               // Sessions before long break (default 4)
  activeTaskId: String?            // Currently linked task
  isRunning: bool
}
```

#### `lib/domain/models/app_settings.dart`

```dart
enum LayoutVariant { modernist, construct }

@freezed
class AppSettings {
  focusDurationMinutes: int        // Default 25
  shortBreakMinutes: int           // Default 5
  longBreakMinutes: int            // Default 15
  sessionsBeforeLongBreak: int     // Default 4
  autoStartBreaks: bool            // Default false
  highContrastMode: bool           // Default false
  layoutVariant: LayoutVariant     // Default modernist
}
```

### 2. Drift database tables

#### `lib/data/database/app_database.dart`

**Tasks table:**
- `id` TEXT PK
- `title` TEXT
- `description` TEXT default ''
- `priority` TEXT (enum as string)
- `status` TEXT (enum as string) default 'pending'
- `estimatedPomodoros` INTEGER default 1
- `completedPomodoros` INTEGER default 0
- `projectName` TEXT nullable
- `createdAt` DATETIME default currentDateAndTime
- `completedAt` DATETIME nullable

**Sessions table:**
- `id` TEXT PK
- `taskId` TEXT nullable, FK → Tasks.id
- `type` TEXT (enum as string)
- `durationSeconds` INTEGER
- `startedAt` DATETIME
- `completedAt` DATETIME nullable
- `wasCompleted` BOOLEAN default false

**Settings table (single-row):**
- `id` INTEGER PK default 1
- `focusDurationMinutes` INTEGER default 25
- `shortBreakMinutes` INTEGER default 5
- `longBreakMinutes` INTEGER default 15
- `sessionsBeforeLongBreak` INTEGER default 4
- `autoStartBreaks` BOOLEAN default false
- `highContrastMode` BOOLEAN default false
- `layoutVariant` TEXT default 'modernist'

### 3. Database class with DAOs

```dart
@DriftDatabase(tables: [Tasks, Sessions, Settings])
class AppDatabase extends _$AppDatabase {
  // schemaVersion = 1

  // Task CRUD
  getAllTasks() → Future<List<Task>>
  getTasksByStatus(TaskStatus) → Future<List<Task>>
  getActiveTask() → Future<Task?>  // status == active
  insertTask(companion) → Future<void>
  updateTask(companion) → Future<void>
  deleteTask(id) → Future<void>
  incrementTaskPomodoros(taskId) → Future<void>  // completedPomodoros += 1

  // Session CRUD
  insertSession(companion) → Future<void>
  updateSession(companion) → Future<void>
  getSessionsInRange(start, end) → Future<List<Session>>  // For stats
  getSessionsForTask(taskId) → Future<List<Session>>
  getCompletedFocusSessionCount() → Future<int>
  getTodaysSessions() → Future<List<Session>>
  getWeeklySessions() → Future<List<Session>>  // Last 7 days

  // Settings
  getSettings() → Future<Setting>  // Returns single row, inserts default if missing
  updateSettings(companion) → Future<void>
}
```

Database file opened via `LazyDatabase` using `path_provider`, stored as `bauhaus_pomodoro.sqlite`.

### 4. Repository layer

#### `task_repository.dart`
Thin wrapper over AppDatabase converting Drift rows ↔ domain models.

#### `session_repository.dart`
Handles session creation, completion marking, and range queries.

#### `settings_repository.dart`
Loads settings on app start, persists changes.

### 5. Riverpod providers

```dart
final databaseProvider = Provider<AppDatabase>((ref) => AppDatabase());

final taskRepositoryProvider = Provider((ref) =>
    TaskRepository(ref.watch(databaseProvider)));
final sessionRepositoryProvider = Provider((ref) =>
    SessionRepository(ref.watch(databaseProvider)));
final settingsRepositoryProvider = Provider((ref) =>
    SettingsRepository(ref.watch(databaseProvider)));
```

### 6. Run code generation

```bash
dart run build_runner build --delete-conflicting-outputs
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All freezed models compile and generate correctly
- [ ] Drift database creates tables on first launch
- [ ] Can insert a task and read it back
- [ ] Can insert a session linked to a task
- [ ] Settings table auto-creates default row
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 04: App Shell + Navigation
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 1 — Foundation
**Blocked by:** #02, #03
**Blocks:** #07, #09, #11, #14

---

## Summary

Build the app shell with go_router navigation, the Bauhaus top app bar, bottom navigation bar, and scaffold that wraps all four screens.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Router (`lib/core/router.dart`)

Define a `GoRouter` with `ShellRoute` for the bottom nav:

```dart
final routerProvider = Provider<GoRouter>((ref) {
  return GoRouter(
    initialLocation: '/timer',
    routes: [
      ShellRoute(
        builder: (context, state, child) => AppShell(child: child),
        routes: [
          GoRoute(path: '/timer', builder: (_, __) => const TimerScreen()),
          GoRoute(path: '/tasks', builder: (_, __) => const TasksScreen()),
          GoRoute(path: '/stats', builder: (_, __) => const EfficiencyReportScreen()),
          GoRoute(path: '/settings', builder: (_, __) => const SettingsScreen()),
        ],
      ),
    ],
  );
});
```

### 2. App Shell widget

A `Scaffold` that wraps every screen with:
- `BauhausAppBar` at the top (fixed, 64px height, 4px bottom border)
- `BauhausNavBar` at the bottom (fixed, 80px height, 4px top border)
- `body` is the routed child with appropriate padding (top: 64+24, bottom: 80+24)

### 3. BauhausAppBar

Layout from the mockups:
- Left: hamburger menu icon + "BAUHAUS POMODORO" text
- Right: settings gear icon (navigates to /settings)
- Background: `surface` color
- Bottom border: 4px solid `onSurface`
- Title: Space Grotesk, Black weight, uppercase, tight tracking

### 4. BauhausNavBar

Four navigation items matching the mockups:
- Timer (timer icon) → `/timer`
- Tasks (list_alt icon) → `/tasks`
- Stats (bar_chart icon) → `/stats`
- Profile (person icon) → `/settings`

Active tab indicator: filled circle with `primaryContainer` background, 2px `onSurface` border. Inactive: just the icon in `onSurface`.

### 5. main.dart + app.dart

`main.dart` wraps app in `ProviderScope`.

`app.dart` creates `MaterialApp.router` with:
- `routerConfig` from the router provider
- `theme` from `BauhausTheme.light()`
- `debugShowCheckedModeBanner: false`

### 6. Placeholder screens

Create minimal placeholder widgets for each screen that display the screen name in headline style so navigation can be tested.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] App launches showing timer screen with top and bottom bars
- [ ] Bottom nav switches between all 4 screens
- [ ] Active nav item shows filled circle indicator
- [ ] Top bar displays "BAUHAUS POMODORO" with correct typography
- [ ] Settings gear navigates to settings screen
- [ ] All navigation transitions are instant (no animations — Bauhaus is direct)
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 05: Timer State Machine + Service
Ticket 06: Task CRUD + Repository
Ticket 07: Timer Screen UI
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Screens
**Blocked by:** #04, #05
**Blocks:** #15

---

## Summary

Build the main timer screen matching the Bauhaus mockup: the master timer circle, asymmetric layout, ghost-block controls, session/time metadata, and active task preview.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Screen layout

The timer screen uses an asymmetric two-column layout on tablet/desktop, single column on mobile:

**Left column (or top on mobile): Master Timer Circle**
- A perfect circle (the ONLY round element in the app)
- 4px solid `onSurface` border
- Fill: `primaryContainer` (#FF6347) when in focus phase, `surface` when paused/idle, `tertiaryContainer` when on break
- Ghost block: 6px offset solid circle behind the timer
- Center content: "FOCUS PERIOD" label (10px, Inter, Black, uppercase, wide tracking) + time display (Space Grotesk, 5rem mobile / 8rem desktop, Black weight, tight tracking)

**Right column (or bottom on mobile): Controls + Info**

1. **Header group:** 8px left border in `primary`, headline "DESIGN THE TIME" (or dynamic based on active task), project subtitle in `secondary`
2. **Heavy divider:** 8px full-width `onSurface`
3. **Controls grid:** 12 columns — START button (col-span-8, ghost-block, `primary` bg) + Reset button (col-span-4, ghost-block, `surface` bg with refresh icon)
4. **Meta blocks:** 2-column grid with 4px border — Session counter (e.g. "01/04") on `surfaceContainerHigh` + Total time on `tertiaryContainer`
5. **Task preview:** Active task with checkbox + title, styled as Bauhaus block list items

### 2. Widgets

#### `master_timer_circle.dart`
The circular timer display. Takes `TimerState` and renders:
- The outer circle with ghost block
- Phase label ("FOCUS PERIOD", "SHORT BREAK", "LONG BREAK")
- Formatted time (MM:SS)
- Circular progress indicator using `CustomPainter` with a `primary` arc stroke (4px) overlaying the border

#### `timer_controls.dart`
The START/PAUSE button + Reset button in ghost blocks. START toggles to PAUSE when running. Uses `ref.read(timerProvider.notifier)` for actions.

#### `active_task_preview.dart`
Shows the currently linked task. If no task linked, shows "No task selected" with a button to navigate to /tasks. Checkboxes are square (Bauhaus — no rounded checkboxes).

### 3. State wiring

All state comes from Riverpod:
- `ref.watch(timerProvider)` for timer state
- `ref.watch(formattedTimeProvider)` for the MM:SS string
- `ref.watch(timerProgressProvider)` for circular progress
- `ref.watch(activeTaskProvider)` for the task preview

### 4. Timer phase colors

| Phase | Circle fill | Label |
|-------|------------|-------|
| idle | surface | — |
| focus | primaryContainer | FOCUS PERIOD |
| shortBreak | tertiaryContainer | SHORT BREAK |
| longBreak | secondary | LONG BREAK |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Timer circle renders with ghost block and correct fill colors per phase
- [ ] Time displays in MM:SS format with correct typography
- [ ] START button starts timer, toggles to PAUSE
- [ ] Reset button stops and resets timer
- [ ] Session counter shows current/total (e.g. 01/04)
- [ ] Total accumulated time displays correctly
- [ ] Active task name displays when a task is linked
- [ ] Layout is asymmetric — left-heavy timer, right controls
- [ ] All ghost blocks animate on press
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 08: Timer Notifications + Audio
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 2 — Core Timer
**Blocked by:** #05
**Blocks:** #15

---

## Summary

Add local notifications and sound effects when a Pomodoro phase completes, so the user knows to take a break or resume work even if the app is backgrounded.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Notification service

Use `flutter_local_notifications` to show a notification when:
- Focus session completes → "Focus complete! Time for a break."
- Short break completes → "Break over. Ready to focus?"
- Long break completes → "Long break done. New cycle!"

Android: create a notification channel "Pomodoro Timer" with importance HIGH.
iOS: request notification permissions on first timer start.

### 2. Sound effects

Use `audioplayers` to play a short completion sound:
- Bundle a simple bell/chime sound as an asset (`assets/sounds/complete.mp3`)
- Play on each phase completion
- Respect system volume / do-not-disturb

### 3. Integration with TimerNotifier

Add notification + sound triggers in `_onPhaseComplete()`. The notification service is injected via the constructor.

### 4. Platform setup

**Android:**
- Add notification icon (`ic_notification`) as a simple geometric shape (circle or square — Bauhaus)
- Configure channel in `AndroidManifest.xml`

**iOS:**
- Request provisional notification permission
- Configure `AppDelegate.swift` for local notifications

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Notification appears when focus session completes
- [ ] Notification appears when break completes
- [ ] Completion sound plays on phase transitions
- [ ] Notifications work when app is backgrounded
- [ ] iOS permission request works on first timer start
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 09: Tasks Screen UI
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Screens
**Blocked by:** #04, #06
**Blocks:** #15

---

## Summary

Build the tasks screen matching the Bauhaus mockup: the "staircase grid" asymmetric layout, priority-colored task cards with varying column spans, and the add-task action.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Screen layout

**Header section** (12-col grid, 4px border):
- Left (col-span-8): "CURRENT FOCUS ARCHITECTURE" headline (Space Grotesk, 5xl, Black, uppercase), badges ("Work Cycle" in `secondary` bg, version in `tertiaryContainer` bg)
- Right (col-span-4): Session progress counter (e.g. "04/12"), progress bar (2px border, `primary` fill)

**Staircase grid:**
A 12-column CSS grid where task cards have intentionally varying column spans to create asymmetry:
- High priority tasks: col-span-7 or col-span-8
- Secondary tasks: col-span-5 or col-span-4
- Maintenance tasks: col-span-4
- Active task: col-span-8 with `primaryContainer` background
- Empty blocks: solid `onSurface` fill for asymmetric rhythm (col-span-2)

All cards have 4px `onSurface` borders. The grid has 4px left and top borders, each card contributes right and bottom borders.

### 2. Task Card widget (`task_card.dart`)

Each card shows:
- Priority indicator: small colored square (red for high, blue for secondary, yellow for maintenance)
- Priority label: "PRIORITY 01", "SECONDARY", "MAINTENANCE" (label style, uppercase)
- Title: Space Grotesk, 2xl, bold, uppercase
- Description: Inter, sm, `onSurfaceVariant`
- Footer: pomodoro estimate ("4 Pomodoros Estimated") + action button
- Active card: `primaryContainer` background with running timer display and timer icon

On hover: title translates 2px right (group-hover effect).

### 3. Add Task sheet (`add_task_sheet.dart`)

A bottom sheet (sharp corners, 4px top border) for creating new tasks:
- Title input (2px bottom border, no background — Bauhaus input style)
- Description textarea
- Priority selector: 3 colored squares to tap
- Pomodoro estimate: number stepper
- "ADD CONSTRUCTION TASK" button with ghost-block

### 4. Task interactions

- Tap a task card → set it as active task (links to timer)
- Tap checkbox → mark task complete
- Long press → edit/delete options
- "+ Add Construction Task" button → opens add sheet

### 5. State wiring

```dart
final allTasksProvider = StreamProvider<List<PomodoroTask>>((ref) {
  return ref.watch(taskRepositoryProvider).watchAllTasks();
});
```

Tasks are sorted: active first, then by priority (high → secondary → maintenance), then by creation date.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tasks display in staircase grid with varying column spans
- [ ] Priority colors match: red (high), blue (secondary), yellow (maintenance)
- [ ] Active task highlighted with primaryContainer background
- [ ] Add task sheet creates a new task
- [ ] Tapping a task sets it as the active task
- [ ] Task cards have 4px borders and correct typography
- [ ] Empty asymmetric blocks render for visual rhythm
- [ ] Hover/press interactions work (title shift, ghost-block press)
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 10: Task-Timer Integration
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 3 — Screens
**Blocked by:** #05, #06
**Blocks:** #15

---

## Summary

Connect the task system to the timer so that starting a timer with a linked task tracks pomodoros against that task, and task status updates are reflected in the timer screen's active task preview.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Linking a task to the timer

When a user taps a task card on the tasks screen:
1. Call `taskRepository.setTaskActive(taskId)` — marks it active, deactivates others
2. Call `ref.read(timerProvider.notifier).setActiveTask(taskId)`
3. Navigate to `/timer`

### 2. Pomodoro tracking

When a focus session completes in `TimerNotifier._onPhaseComplete()`:
- If `state.activeTaskId != null`, call `taskRepository.incrementPomodoros(taskId)`
- The task's `completedPomodoros` increments by 1
- If `completedPomodoros >= estimatedPomodoros`, show a subtle indicator (but don't auto-complete — user decides)

### 3. Timer screen updates

The timer screen's header group shows:
- If task linked: task title as headline, project name as subtitle
- If no task: "DESIGN THE TIME" default headline

The active task preview at the bottom shows the linked task's title and pomodoro progress (e.g. "2 of 4 pomodoros").

### 4. Tasks screen updates

The active task card on the tasks screen shows a live timer countdown when the timer is running, using `ref.watch(formattedTimeProvider)`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tapping a task navigates to timer with that task linked
- [ ] Completing a focus session increments the task's pomodoro count
- [ ] Timer screen headline reflects active task name
- [ ] Task preview shows pomodoro progress
- [ ] Active task card on tasks screen shows live timer when running
- [ ] Unlinking a task (reset) clears the active task
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 11: Statistics Service + Session Aggregation
Ticket 12: Efficiency Report Screen UI
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 4 — Analytics
**Blocked by:** #04, #11
**Blocks:** #15

---

## Summary

Build the efficiency report screen matching the Bauhaus mockup: focus distribution bar chart, key metrics sidebar, tomato circle grid, and velocity map with asymmetric blocks.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Screen layout

**Header:**
- "EFFICIENCY REPORT" — headline with 8px underline
- "DATA CYCLE: [date range]" — label in `primary`

**Main grid** (12 columns):

#### Focus Distribution Chart (col-span-8)
- Ghost-shadow card with 4px border
- Bar chart using `fl_chart` `BarChart` widget
- 7 bar groups (Mon-Sun), each with 2 stacked bars:
  - Bottom bar: `primary` (Deep Work)
  - Top bar: `secondary` (Logistics)
- Y-axis: faint 2px grid lines at 20% opacity
- X-axis: day labels (MON, TUE, ...) in label style
- Legend: two small colored squares with labels
- **Chart styling:** flat tops (no rounding), 2px borders on each bar segment

#### Sidebar metrics (col-span-4)

**Total Cycles card:**
- Ghost-shadow, 4px border
- "TOTAL CYCLES" label in `secondary`
- Large number (Space Grotesk, 5xl, Black)
- Divider + "+N% vs last week" label

**Tomatoes Harvested card:**
- `tertiaryContainer` background, 4px border, ghost-shadow
- "TOMATOES HARVESTED" headline
- 5×2 grid of perfect circles:
  - Completed: `primary` fill, 2px border
  - Remaining: 10% opacity `onSurface`, 2px border
- "08 / 10 Goal" label

### 2. Velocity Map section

Below the main grid, an asymmetric 4-column grid:

| Block | Span | Height | Background | Content |
|-------|------|--------|-----------|--------|
| Avg Session | 1 col | 32 | surfaceContainerHighest | "28m" |
| Peak Time | 1 col | 48 (row-span-2) | primary | "09:15 AM" (white) |
| Break Compliance | 1 col | 32 | secondary | "94%" (white) |
| Interruption Rate | 1 col | 32 | surfaceContainerHighest | "LOW" |
| Daily Streak | 2 col | 32 | tertiary | "12" + star icon (white) |

All blocks: 4px `onSurface` border, label at top, value at bottom, Space Grotesk for values.

### 3. Chart implementation with fl_chart

```dart
BarChart(
  BarChartData(
    barGroups: weeklyData.entries.map((e) => BarChartGroupData(
      x: e.key,
      barRods: [
        BarChartRodData(
          toY: e.value.total,
          rodStackItems: [
            BarChartRodStackItem(0, e.value.deepWork, BauhausColors.primary),
            BarChartRodStackItem(e.value.deepWork, e.value.total, BauhausColors.secondary),
          ],
          width: 24,
          borderRadius: BorderRadius.zero,  // Sharp tops!
          borderSide: BorderSide(width: 2, color: BauhausColors.onSurface),
        ),
      ],
    )).toList(),
    // ... grid lines, titles config
  ),
)
```

### 4. State wiring

All data comes from the statistics providers (ticket #11). Use `ref.watch` with `AsyncValue` pattern — show loading state as empty blocks with subtle pulse animation.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Bar chart renders weekly focus distribution with stacked bars
- [ ] Bars have flat tops and 2px borders (no rounding)
- [ ] Total cycles displays with week-over-week change
- [ ] Tomato grid shows completed vs remaining as circles
- [ ] Velocity map renders asymmetric blocks with all metrics
- [ ] Peak time block is double-height (row-span-2)
- [ ] Daily streak shows star icon
- [ ] All ghost-shadows render correctly
- [ ] Data refreshes when navigating to the screen
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 13: Settings State + Persistence
Ticket 14: Settings Screen UI
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 5 — Settings + Polish
**Blocked by:** #04, #13
**Blocks:** #15

---

## Summary

Build the settings screen matching the Bauhaus mockup: sidebar navigation, timer parameters with custom inputs, visual interface section with theme toggle and layout selector, and the danger-zone reset button.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Screen layout

**Header:**
- 4px left accent bar in `primary`
- "SETTINGS" — headline (Space Grotesk, 5xl, Black, uppercase)
- "SYSTEM CONFIGURATION / V2.0.4" — label in `secondary`

**Main grid** (12 columns):
- Sidebar (col-span-3, hidden on mobile): sticky nav links — General, Audio, Focus, Account. Active link: `primary` color with 4px underline. Inactive: 40% opacity.
- Content (col-span-9): settings sections

### 2. Timer Parameters section

Section header: "TIMER PARAMETERS" headline + "CONFIG_01" badge (white on black). 4px divider with 1/4 width `primary` accent.

#### Focus Duration row
- Ghost-shadow card with 2px border
- Left: "Focus Duration" label + description
- Right: number input (2px bottom border, Space Grotesk bold, centered) + "MIN" label
- Input styling: `surfaceContainer` bg on focus, `primary` bottom border on focus

#### Auto-Start Breaks row
- 2px border card
- Left: label + description
- Right: `BauhausToggle` widget

### 3. Visual Interface section

Section header: "VISUAL INTERFACE" + "UI_VIS_02" badge. Divider with 2/3 width `secondary` accent.

#### High Contrast Mode
- Toggle row identical to auto-start breaks
- Toggle in "on" state: full `onSurface` background, `tertiaryContainer` indicator

#### Layout Selector
- 2-column grid of selectable cards:
  - **Modernist** (selected): 4px `primary` border, check_circle icon top-right, preview thumbnails (1/3 black + 2/3 grey blocks)
  - **Construct** (unselected): 2px `onSurface` border, 40% opacity, different block arrangement preview

### 4. Danger Zone

"RESET SYSTEM STATE" button:
- Full-width, 2px `error` border, `error` text
- On hover: `error` background, `onError` text
- Trash icon right-aligned
- Warning text below: "WARNING: ALL SESSION HISTORICAL DATA WILL BE PURGED."
- On tap: show confirmation dialog (Bauhaus-styled: sharp corners, heavy borders)

### 5. State wiring

All settings bound to `ref.watch(settingsProvider)`. Changes call the repository update methods. Toggle states animate with the `BauhausToggle` widget.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Timer parameters section renders with correct inputs
- [ ] Focus duration input updates settings on change
- [ ] Auto-start breaks toggle works
- [ ] High contrast mode toggle works
- [ ] Layout selector shows Modernist/Construct with selection state
- [ ] Sidebar nav renders on tablet/desktop, hidden on mobile
- [ ] Reset button shows confirmation before resetting
- [ ] All borders and typography match Bauhaus design system
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes

jobs.title: Ticket 15: Integration Testing + QA Polish
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 5 — Settings + Polish
**Blocked by:** #07, #08, #09, #10, #12, #14
**Blocks:** #16

---

## Summary

End-to-end integration testing of all flows, fix visual inconsistencies, and ensure the full app works as a cohesive Bauhaus experience.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Integration tests

Write widget tests for each critical flow:

#### Timer flow
- Start timer → verify countdown begins
- Complete focus session → verify transition to break
- Complete 4 sessions → verify long break triggers
- Pause and resume → verify time is preserved
- Reset → verify return to idle state

#### Task flow
- Create task → verify it appears in list
- Set task active → verify it links to timer
- Complete pomodoro → verify task count increments
- Mark task complete → verify status changes

#### Stats flow
- Complete several sessions → verify stats update
- Check bar chart data matches session records
- Verify streak calculation

#### Settings flow
- Change focus duration → verify timer reflects new duration
- Toggle auto-start breaks → verify behavior changes
- Reset settings → verify defaults restored

### 2. Visual QA checklist

- [ ] Zero border-radius on ALL components (except timer circle + nav active)
- [ ] All borders are minimum 2px
- [ ] Ghost blocks render with correct offset on all cards/buttons
- [ ] Space Grotesk used for all headlines, Inter for body/labels
- [ ] All headline text is uppercase
- [ ] Color consistency: no off-palette colors anywhere
- [ ] No default Material shadows anywhere (check elevation: 0)
- [ ] Bottom nav active indicator is a perfect circle
- [ ] Task grid has correct asymmetric column spans
- [ ] Bar chart bars have flat tops

### 3. Responsive behavior

- Mobile: single-column layouts, stacked sections
- Tablet: two-column asymmetric layouts where designed
- Bottom nav visible on all screen sizes (hidden on desktop in settings only for sidebar)

### 4. Performance

- Timer tick does not cause unnecessary rebuilds (use `select` on providers)
- Database queries are efficient (proper indices on frequently queried columns)
- Chart rendering is smooth (no jank on 60fps)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] All integration tests pass
- [ ] Full timer cycle works: focus → break → focus → ... → long break
- [ ] Tasks integrate with timer correctly
- [ ] Stats reflect actual session data
- [ ] Settings changes take effect immediately
- [ ] Visual QA checklist all items pass
- [ ] No `flutter analyze` warnings
- [ ] App builds cleanly on Android

jobs.title: Ticket 16: Theme Variants + High Contrast Mode
jobs.body:
**Phase:** 5 — Settings + Polish
**Blocked by:** #02, #13, #15

---

## Summary

Implement the two layout variants (Modernist and Construct) and high contrast mode that the user can toggle in settings.

## Implementation Details

### 1. Modernist variant (default)

The current design as specified in the CLAUDE.md and mockups. This is the base theme.

### 2. Construct variant

An alternative layout approach that inverts the asymmetry:
- Timer screen: controls on left, timer circle on right
- Tasks screen: wider right columns instead of left
- Stats screen: sidebar metrics on left, chart on right
- Different block-arrangement preview in settings selector

Implement as a `LayoutVariant` enum consumed by screen widgets. Each screen checks `ref.watch(settingsProvider).layoutVariant` and adjusts column ordering.

### 3. High Contrast Mode

When enabled:
- Background becomes pure `#FFFFFF`
- Surface containers become `#F0F0F0` / `#E0E0E0` (higher contrast steps)
- All borders become 4px minimum (up from 2px)
- Text color becomes pure `#000000`
- Primary stays `#B12C16` (already high contrast)
- Ghost blocks become more pronounced (6px offset instead of 4px)

Implement as a separate `BauhausTheme.highContrast()` factory that returns a modified `ThemeData`. The app switches themes reactively based on the setting.

### 4. Theme provider

```dart
final themeProvider = Provider<ThemeData>((ref) {
  final settings = ref.watch(settingsProvider).valueOrNull;
  if (settings?.highContrastMode == true) {
    return BauhausTheme.highContrast();
  }
  return BauhausTheme.light();
});
```

`MaterialApp.router` consumes this provider for its `theme` parameter.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Modernist layout is the default
- [ ] Switching to Construct variant rearranges screen layouts
- [ ] High contrast mode increases border weights and contrast
- [ ] Theme switches reactively without app restart
- [ ] Settings screen layout selector shows correct selection state
- [ ] `flutter analyze` passes
